metadata.career_relevance: Retries with exponential backoff are the standard pattern for transient network failures. Every Razorpay HTTP call to Visa/Mastercard uses retries — 30% of failures are transient and succeed on retry. Rate limiters protect downstream APIs from being overwhelmed by your service. After this topic, you handle transient failures transparently and respect external API rate limits.

hook.what_you_will_build: By the end, you'll add @Retry with exponential backoff, configure retries only for specific exception types, combine @Retry with @CircuitBreaker, and add @RateLimiter to respect downstream API quotas.

visual_aid.description: Two panels: (1) @Retry timeline for a transient failure: T=0 attempt 1 fails (red X), wait 100ms, T=100ms attempt 2 fails (red X), wait 200ms, T=300ms attempt 3 succeeds (green checkmark). Total user-visible latency: 300ms. Compare: without retry: T=0 fail → user sees error. (2) @RateLimiter token bucket: bucket of 100 tokens refills every 1 second. Each call consumes 1 token. When bucket empty: new calls wait up to 500ms (timeoutDuration) for a token. After 500ms: RequestNotPermittedException → fallback (drop/queue).
visual_aid.alt_text: Retry timeline showing 3 attempts with exponential backoff, and RateLimiter token bucket with permit acquisition

worked_example.scenario: Zerodha's order router calls the NSE trading engine. Network blips are common during market open. Add retry with exponential backoff for transient errors, combined with a circuit breaker for sustained failures.
worked_example.problem_statement:
1. application.yml: retry (3 attempts, 100ms, exponential) + circuit breaker
2. OrderRouterService: @CircuitBreaker + @Retry on submitOrder()
3. Retry only IOException and ConnectException — not InvalidOrderException
4. Test: mock to fail twice then succeed — verify 1 success from 3 attempts

step_by_step_solution.details:
```yaml
resilience4j:
  retry:
    instances:
      nse-gateway:
        maxAttempts: 3
        waitDuration: 100ms
        enableExponentialBackoff: true
        exponentialBackoffMultiplier: 2.0
        retryExceptions:
          - java.io.IOException
          - java.net.ConnectException
        ignoreExceptions:
          - com.zerodha.InvalidOrderException
  circuitbreaker:
    instances:
      nse-gateway:
        slidingWindowSize: 10
        failureRateThreshold: 40
        minimumNumberOfCalls: 5
        waitDurationInOpenState: 30s
```

worked_example.expected_output:
# Test: fail twice, succeed third:
[RETRY] Attempt 1/3 → IOException → retry in 100ms
[RETRY] Attempt 2/3 → IOException → retry in 200ms
[RETRY] Attempt 3/3 → SUCCESS
OrderAck{status=ACCEPTED}

# Test: fail 3 times → fallback:
OrderAck{status=REJECTED, reason=NSE_UNAVAILABLE}
things_to_notice: Annotation order: @CircuitBreaker OUTSIDE @Retry. The CB wraps the retried operation as ONE call. If all 3 retries fail: the CB records 1 failure. Wrong order (retry outside CB): each retry counts as a separate CB failure → circuit opens 3× faster.

ignoreExceptions: InvalidOrderException should NOT be retried — it's a permanent business error (wrong symbol, insufficient margin). Retrying it 3 times wastes time and logs. Only transient infrastructure errors (IOException, ConnectException) deserve retry.

exponentialBackoffMultiplier=2.0: attempt 1 immediately, attempt 2 after 100ms, attempt 3 after 200ms. This prevents thundering herd when many services retry simultaneously — staggered timing reduces server load.

maxAttempts=3: 'total attempts' includes the first try. maxAttempts=3 means: 1 original + 2 retries. Many developers expect maxAttempts=3 to mean 3 retries (4 total attempts) — verify the semantics in your Resilience4j version.

industry_examples.use_case: Visa/Mastercard connections fail transiently under load. Razorpay retries up to 3 times with 100ms exponential backoff. 30% of failures succeed on first retry. retryExceptions: IOException, SSLException. ignoreExceptions: InvalidCardException, CardDeclinedException (permanent — retrying won't help).

@RateLimiter on NSE API calls: SEBI-mandated rate limit of 200 orders/second. limitForPeriod=200, limitRefreshPeriod=1s. If Zerodha's own order router exceeds 200 orders/second, @RateLimiter queues excess requests for up to 500ms before throwing RequestNotPermittedException.

applications.interview_relevance: 'When would you use retry vs circuit breaker?' → Retry: transient failures (network blip, restart). Circuit breaker: sustained failures (service down). Use both together: retry handles blips, CB handles outages. Order: @CircuitBreaker wraps @Retry.

common_gaps.what_students_get_wrong: Not configuring ignoreExceptions — retrying permanent business errors (InvalidCard, InsufficientFunds) wastes time and produces confusing logs.
common_gaps.remediation: Always configure retryExceptions (whitelist) or ignoreExceptions (blacklist). For payment systems: retry only IOException, ConnectException, SocketTimeoutException. Never retry: InvalidCardException, CardDeclinedException, InsufficientFundsException. The user's card is genuinely invalid — retrying 3 times doesn't fix it.
common_gaps.detection_pattern: Logs show 3 retry attempts for InvalidCardException; 300ms delay for a permanent business error

common_gaps.what_students_get_wrong: @Retry outside @CircuitBreaker — each retry counts as a separate CB failure, opening the circuit 3× faster than expected.
common_gaps.remediation: Order: @CircuitBreaker (outer) → @Retry (inner). The CB wraps the retried operation and counts it as one call, regardless of how many retries happened. If all 3 retries fail: CB records 1 failure. With wrong order: 3 retries = 3 CB failures → circuit opens in 1/3 the expected time.
common_gaps.detection_pattern: Circuit opens much faster than configured threshold suggests; CB records failures at 3× expected rate

key_takeaways: Retry: handles transient failures (network blip) — retries transparently
@Retry(maxAttempts=3): 1 original + 2 retries (read docs to confirm semantics)
exponentialBackoff: prevents thundering herd on mass failures
retryExceptions: whitelist — only retry these exception types
ignoreExceptions: blacklist — never retry these (permanent errors)
@RateLimiter: limits your outgoing call rate to protect downstream
timeoutDuration: wait up to N ms for a rate limit permit
@CircuitBreaker OUTSIDE @Retry: CB wraps retry sequence as one call
Wrong order (retry outside CB): 3× faster circuit opening than intended

self_check_questions.question: maxAttempts=3, fails all 3. How many failures does the circuit breaker record?
self_check_questions.answer: 1 failure (when @CircuitBreaker wraps @Retry). The CB sees the entire retry sequence as one operation. If all 3 attempts fail, the CB records exactly 1 failure. This is the correct behavior — otherwise one outage would trip the circuit 3× faster.

reflection.next_topic_preview: Next: Bulkhead isolation — prevents one overloaded feature from consuming all threads and starving other features.
